title: Prove BinaryMerkleRoot depth parameter cannot be manipulated to accept invalid proofs
description: The `merkleProofLength` input in all three circuits controls how many siblings are actually used by BinaryMerkleRoot, but there is no constraint that `merkleProofLength` matches the expected tree depth. An attacker could supply a shorter proof path against a subtree root that happens to be in the root history buffer. Write a formal property (Certora-style invariant or circom assert) proving that for any accepted witness, the Merkle root output corresponds to a tree of exactly the deployed depth, or add an equality constraint `merkleProofLength === EXPECTED_DEPTH` to each circuit.
